article_title: Ore Energy Signs Europe's Largest Iron-Air Battery Deal, Committing 1 GWh of Multi-Day Storage to Dutch Supplier Budget Thuis
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

content_preview.title: Ore Energy Signs Europe's Largest Iron-Air Battery Deal, Committing 1 GWh of Multi-Day Storage to Dutch Supplier Budget Thuis
content_preview.summary: Amsterdam startup Ore Energy will deploy up to 1 GWh of 100-hour iron-air storage for Budget Thuis, with a 400 MWh first phase due in 2028.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Amsterdam-based startup Ore Energy has signed an agreement to deploy up to 1 gigawatt-hour of iron-air long-duration energy storage for Dutch energy supplier Budget Thuis, with a committed first phase of 400 megawatt-hours scheduled for delivery in 2028, [according to Energy-Storage.News](https://www.energy-storage.news/multi-day-energy-storage-startup-ore-energy-signs-1gwh-iron-air-battery-deal-with-dutch-utility/). [POWER Magazine](https://www.powermag.com/ore-energy-will-deploy-1...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Tight, well-structured News piece (516 words, within the 400-1200 News range). Clear Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know framing. Technical depth is appropriate and accurate; the rusting/unrusting chemistry is described correctly.
editor_notes.source_verification: All five source snapshots read from disk and verified. source-0 (Energy-Storage.News, status 200): confirms up to 1,000 MWh / 1 GWh, Budget Thuis 400 MWh first phase in 2028, 24-100 hour discharge configurability, MWh-scale unit in 40-foot enclosure, and 'Ore Energy was founded in 2022' — but does NOT mention TU Delft. source-1 (Electrek, 200): confirms verbatim the Yilmaz curtailment quote and the Buitelaar 'reducing exposure to volatile fossil fuel prices...' quote, 100-hour storage, 24-100 hour range, 40-foot containers, iron/water/air, European supply chain avoiding critical minerals, and the EDF pilot (Europe's first grid-connected iron-air LDES pilot, Aug-Nov 2025, up to four days). source-2 (ESS News, 200): confirms verbatim the Yilmaz 'iron-air will become as important for wind as lithium-ion has been for solar' quote and 'Budget Thuis, part of the Nuts Groep and one of the largest energy suppliers in the Netherlands with more than 1 million customers.' source-3 (POWER Magazine, 200): confirms verbatim 'the largest iron-air energy storage offtake in continental Europe to date and the first with a European energy supplier' — the article correctly attributes this to POWER and frames it as POWER's characterization, not absolute fact. source-4 (Silicon Canals, 200): confirms the rusting/oxidation chemistry, the EUR10M stealth-exit funding from Positron Ventures and SPRIND, and 'Ore Energy, a science startup spun out of TU Delft.'
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every number, name, date, and quote traces to a cited source and is verbatim where quoted. The two superlatives are correctly hedged as POWER Magazine's description and are not overstated as absolute fact. One clause-level misattribution: the sentence 'Ore Energy is a science startup spun out of TU Delft and was founded in 2022, [according to Energy-Storage.News]' bundles two facts under the Energy-Storage.News cite, but the 'spun out of TU Delft' clause is supported by Silicon Canals (also cited in the article), not by Energy-Storage.News; only the 'founded in 2022' half appears in Energy-Storage.News. Both facts are true and both outlets are cited; the misattribution is of a single clause and is honestly summarizable in a corrections note.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. No sensationalism, no editorializing, no AI self-reference. Superlatives are attributed rather than asserted.
editor_notes.originality: Distinct from prior coverage. The Mar 2 Google/Form Energy 30 GWh Minnesota iron-air article covers a different company, country, and customer; the May 12 Chinese Academy of Sciences all-iron FLOW battery article is lab research on a different chemistry. This is a new European (Dutch) energy-supplier offtake. The article appropriately mentions Form Energy as the other iron-air player without duplicating the Minnesota story.
concerns: Clause-level misattribution: 'spun out of TU Delft' is attributed to Energy-Storage.News but is sourced from Silicon Canals (source-4); Energy-Storage.News supports only the 'founded in 2022' portion of that sentence. Both facts are accurate and both outlets are cited. Filed as a correction.
recommendations: Non-blocking: powermag.com and siliconcanals.com are not in config/source_allowlist.txt. Both are reputable industry/tech outlets and snapshots returned HTTP 200. Consider adding them to the allowlist in a future maintenance pass. Allowlist file not modified per policy.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively accurate, neutral, and original News piece with all quotes verbatim and all specifics traceable. The only defect is a single-clause source misattribution (TU Delft spinout cited to Energy-Storage.News instead of Silicon Canals), which a corrections note covers honestly without affecting the headline, summary, or lead. APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS.
